rag_rules: Fix type mismatches between component ports and their assumed/guaranteed values by aligning declarations with visible AADL feature types.
Ensure AGREE contracts refer only to visible identifiers and avoid package-qualified names not present in the model.
Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they involve component ports.
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations in AGREE.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without relying on implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE expressions are either visible AADL features or locally declared helpers.
Ensure type consistency between assumed values and actual port types in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use nested 'if ... then ... else' for branching logic in AGREE.
Use AGREE logical operators like 'and', 'or', 'not(...)', and implication '=>' for complex expressions.
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE that are not declared in the AADL model.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
